title: System Design Fundamentals
learningObjectives: Defend **System design fundamentals** choices with trade-offs, failure domains, and metrics
Contrast patterns and anti-patterns interviewers probe with follow-up questions
Sketch boundaries, data flows, and consistency models on a whiteboard
Relate scalability, cost, and operational complexity to team constraints
Use structured responses: requirements, options, decision, verification
Prepare STAR-style stories where architecture decisions had measurable impact

sections.title: Why System Design Fundamentals matters
sections.content: **Fundamentals** are the **scaffold** for every **URL** **shortener** and **order** **service**. Master **latency**, **consistency**, and **failure** **isolation** before **fancy** **diagrams**.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - System Design Fundamentals
sections.content:
### Day 81 — **System** **design** **fundamentals**

### 1. Requirements
**Functional**, **non-functional**, **scale** **assumptions**.

### 2. Building blocks
**LB**, **cache**, **DB**, **queue**, **search**.

### 3. Data
**Sharding**, **replication**, **hot** **keys**.

### 4. Reliability
**Retries**, **timeouts**, **bulkheads**, **degradation**.

### 5. Security
**AuthN/Z**, **rate** **limits**, **data** **minimization**.

### 6. Story
**Black** **Friday**—**edge** **rate** **limit** + **queue** **checkout** **shedding**.

sections.title: Basic — Non-functional four
Intermediate — Stateless tier
sections.output: stateless app servers behind load balancer + shared data tier

sections.title: Advanced — CAP shorthand
sections.output: partition happens: pick consistency vs availability per operation

sections.title: Client to data path
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 81 — Path
User -> DNS
DNS -> LB
LB -> App
App -> Cache
App -> DB
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — System Design Fundamentals (Day 81 assignment)
sections.problem: Print **exactly** one line: edge: cheap rejection; app: domain-aware limits

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: **Vertical** vs **horizontal** **scale**?
conceptual.answer: Bigger machine vs more machines—stateless tiers favor horizontal with load balancers. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **System design fundamentals**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Bigger machine vs more machines—stateless tiers favor horizontal with load balancers. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Bigger machine vs more machines—stateless tiers favor horizontal with load balancers. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

conceptual.question: **Strong** vs **eventual** **read** **path**?
conceptual.answer: User expects immediate truth vs acceptable lag—pick per feature with product buy-in. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **System design fundamentals**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. User expects immediate truth vs acceptable lag—pick per feature with product buy-in. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

conceptual.question: **CDN** **role**?
conceptual.answer: Edge caching for static and cacheable dynamic assets; origin shielding and TLS termination. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Edge caching for static and cacheable dynamic assets; origin shielding and TLS termination. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

conceptual.question: **Rate** **limit** **edge** vs **app**?
conceptual.answer: Edge cheaply rejects abuse; app enforces business rules and per-tenant fairness. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

**(4) Prevention** Add **canary** gate and **diff** **alerts** on **golden** metrics. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. Avoid **blaming** **users**; focus on **measurable** recovery criteria. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Topic | Recall |
|---|---|
| Scale | H vs V |
| LB | Health checks |
| Cache | TTL invalidate |
| CDN | Edge static |
| Queue | Async decouple |
| CAP | P assumed |
| SLO | Error budget |
| Shard | Key choice |
| Hot key | Salting |
| DR | Multi-region |
